title: CEMS Data Quality Problems

metadata.title: CEMS Data Quality Problems
metadata.typical_impact: Invalid emissions data, substitute data penalties, reduced data availability, regulatory non-compliance

description: Five-step procedure for diagnosing and resolving CEMS data quality problems in cement plant continuous emission monitoring systems.

steps.question: Which CEMS parameter is affected and what type of data quality issue is occurring?
steps.action: Determine which analyzer or parameter is affected. Check daily calibration error reports for drift exceedances. Review DAHS for flagged data or error codes. Compare CEMS readings to expected values based on process conditions.
check: Determine which analyzer or parameter is affected (SO2, NOx, CO, THC, HCl, Hg, O2, flow, opacity)
Check daily calibration error reports for drift exceedances (>2.5% of span)
Review DAHS (Data Acquisition and Handling System) for flagged data or error codes
Compare CEMS readings to expected values based on process conditions
Check if issue is intermittent or continuous (time-series analysis)
Determine when the problem started and correlate with any maintenance or process changes
steps.expected_outcome: Clear identification of which parameter, what type of issue, and when it started

steps.question: What is the root cause of the analyzer malfunction?
steps.action: Perform manual calibration check with certified cylinder gases. Check sample transport system. Verify probe condition and sample extraction. Inspect optical components for contamination.
check: Perform manual calibration check with certified cylinder gases
Check sample transport system: heated lines for condensation, sample pump operation, filter condition
Verify probe condition and sample extraction (blockage, corrosion, misalignment)
Check for interference gases affecting analyzer response (cross-sensitivity)
Inspect optical components for contamination (lenses, mirrors, measurement cells)
Verify analyzer environmental conditions (shelter temperature, humidity)
Check electrical connections and communication between analyzer and DAHS
steps.expected_outcome: Root cause of analyzer malfunction identified

steps.question: Have repairs been completed and the analyzer recalibrated successfully?
steps.action: Replace failed components. Clean optical components and sample pathways. Replace calibration gases if expired. Perform full recalibration per manufacturer procedure. Verify response time meets specification.
check: Replace failed components (sample pump, filter, heated line, detector)
Clean optical components and sample pathways
Replace calibration gases if expired or suspect
Perform full recalibration per manufacturer procedure
Verify response time meets performance specification
Run calibration error test to confirm acceptable drift
Document all repairs and parts replaced
steps.expected_outcome: Analyzer returned to proper operation and calibrated

steps.question: Has the invalid data period been properly managed with substitute data procedures?
steps.description: Validate Data and Manage Missing Data
steps.action: Determine the period of invalid data. Apply substitute data procedures per 40 CFR 75 or permit requirements. Calculate impact on data availability percentage.
check: Determine the period of invalid data (start and end timestamps)
Apply substitute data procedures per 40 CFR 75 or permit requirements
Calculate impact on data availability percentage
Determine if data availability will fall below 95% threshold
Document data validation decisions and substitute data methodology used
Update DAHS with corrected flags and data quality codes
steps.expected_outcome: Data gaps properly managed with substitute data procedures applied

steps.question: What preventive measures are needed to avoid repeat CEMS failures?
steps.action: Update preventive maintenance schedule based on failure mode. Ensure adequate spare parts inventory. Review CEMS operator training. Consider adding redundant analyzers for critical parameters.
check: Update preventive maintenance schedule based on failure mode
Ensure adequate spare parts inventory for CEMS components
Review CEMS operator training and troubleshooting procedures
Consider adding redundant analyzers for critical parameters
Implement daily CEMS performance review by qualified personnel
Schedule next QA/QC activity (CGA or RATA) to revalidate system performance
steps.expected_outcome: Preventive measures in place to avoid repeat CEMS failures

calibration_drift.quick_fix: Clean optics, verify cal gas, adjust shelter HVAC

low_data_availability.quick_fix: Improve PM schedule, add UPS, ensure spare analyzer available

biased_readings.quick_fix: Leak check sample system, clean probe, verify cal gas certificates

RATA_failure.quick_fix: Recalibrate, evaluate sampling location, schedule re-RATA
